# Iknaio Cryptoasset Analytics

> Iknaio Cryptoasset Analytics GmbH is a Vienna-based deep-tech company building cryptoasset investigation tools for law enforcement, compliance teams, and professional advisors. We trace blockchain transactions, generate forensic reports, and reveal cross-case connections — built on a decade of peer-reviewed research and the open-source GraphSense platform.

Iknaio is used by 2,500+ investigators across 23+ countries. Customers include law-enforcement agencies, banks and crypto exchanges (for AML screening), and lawyers, tax advisors, and security consultants. Infrastructure is EU-hosted with GDPR-compliant data handling.

## What Iknaio does

- **Pathfinder**: Cryptoasset payment-flow analysis. Whole-network tracing across Bitcoin, Ethereum, Tron, and other supported chains.
- **CaseConnect**: Collaborative investigation platform that surfaces shared insights across related cases.
- **QuickLock**: Automates generation of forensic transaction-tracing reports, removing repetitive manual analysis.
- **API & MCP**: REST API and Model Context Protocol (MCP) interface for integration into AML, case-management, analytics stacks, and AI agent pipelines.
- **Tailored Reports**: Custom report templates with full provenance of underlying data points.
- **Trainings**: On-site and remote training in cryptoasset investigation, compliance, and reporting workflows.

## Platform pages

- [Platform overview](https://iknaio.com/platform/): Pathfinder, CaseConnect, QuickLock, API & MCP, and integration details.
- [Solutions](https://iknaio.com/solutions/): Use cases by audience — Investigate (law enforcement), Comply (financial institutions), Advise (lawyers, tax advisors, consultants).
- [Pricing](https://iknaio.com/pricing/): Free, Starter, Professional, and Institutional tiers with feature matrix and FAQ.
- [About](https://iknaio.com/about/): Company background, team, mission, and timeline (founded 2021 as a spin-off of the Complexity Science Hub Vienna; roots in the 2015 GraphSense research project at AIT Austrian Institute of Technology).
- [Contact](https://iknaio.com/contact/): Demo request and direct contact — contact@iknaio.com.

## Technical research and methodology (insights)

- [Operation Alice](https://iknaio.com/insights/operation-alice/): Case study — supporting the takedown of a darknet CSAM platform (373,000 domains, 5,170 Bitcoin addresses, ~440 suspects identified).
- [Change Address Heuristics](https://iknaio.com/insights/change-detection/): How Iknaio identifies the change output of a Bitcoin transaction, and why it matters for tracing accuracy.
- [Bitcoin Address Types](https://iknaio.com/insights/address-types/): What Bitcoin address types reveal to an investigator and how Iknaio uses them.
- [Blockchain C2](https://iknaio.com/insights/blockchain-c2/): Detection of malware command-and-control channels operating over the blockchain.
- [Cross-chain key reuse](https://iknaio.com/insights/cross-chain-kexy-reuse/): Linking activity across chains via reused cryptographic keys.
- [Tracing DEXs, bridges, cross-chain clusters](https://iknaio.com/insights/tracing-dexs-bridges-cross-chain-clusters/): Methods for following funds through decentralised exchanges and bridges.
- [Navigating the EU crypto regime](https://iknaio.com/insights/navigating-the-eu-crypto-regime/): Compliance overview for MiCA, TFR, and related EU regulations.
- [The crime landscape in DeFi](https://iknaio.com/insights/the-crime-landscape-in-defi/): Patterns of illicit activity in decentralised finance.
- [Improving cryptoasset investigations by connecting cases](https://iknaio.com/insights/improving-cryptoasset-investigations-by-connecting-cases/): The research behind CaseConnect.

## Why Iknaio

- **Research-backed**: 10+ years of peer-reviewed research; 26+ academic publications; every algorithm grounded in published methodology.
- **Whole-network analysis**: Reveals patterns across entire networks rather than wallet-by-wallet, surfacing connections that isolated tools miss.
- **API-first and open source**: Full scriptable API; open-source foundations under MIT license; results are reproducible.
- **EU data sovereignty**: EU-hosted infrastructure, GDPR-compliant, no non-EU subpoena risk; data stays in Europe.
- **Complementary to existing tools**: Built to be the automation and scale layer on top of other analytics tools, not a replacement.
